title: Comprehensive Leveling Translation Table — SAP → FAANG → Microsoft → Netflix → Stripe → Snowflake
description: The definitive leveling translation table mapping SAP career levels to all major FAANG and big-tech companies. Covers Google (L3-L8), Meta (E3-E8), Amazon (L4-L8), Apple (ICT2-ICT7), Microsoft (59-69), Netflix (IC3-IC6), Stripe (L1-L6), Snowflake (IC1-IC7), and SAP's own career ladder. Includes title equivalents, key differentiators per level, comp ranges, and interview expectations per level.

description.value:
How to read this table: SAP career ladders vary by region and organization (SAP Global vs SAP Labs vs SAP Services). The table below maps the SAP 'equivalent' title to each company's level. 'Staff+' roles are highlighted. TC (Total Compensation) ranges are for US-based roles (San Francisco / Seattle / New York).

LevelSAP EquivalentGoogleMetaAmazonAppleMicrosoftNetflixStripeSnowflakeTC Range (USD)
EntryAssociate DeveloperL3E3L4ICT259-60—L1IC1$110-160K
MidDeveloper / ConsultantL4E4L5ICT361-62—L2IC2$150-230K
SeniorSenior Developer / Senior ConsultantL5E5L6 (SDE3)ICT463-64IC3L3IC3$230-350K
StaffSolution Architect / Lead DeveloperL6E6L7 (Principal)ICT565-66IC4L4IC4-IC5$350-550K
Senior StaffEnterprise Architect / Senior ArchitectL7E7L8 (Sr. Principal)ICT667-68IC5L5IC6$550-900K
PrincipalChief Architect / VP EngineeringL8E8L10 (Distinguished)ICT769+IC6L6IC7$900K – $2M+

sections.title: What Each Level Expects — The Staff+ Distinction

description.value:
Staff+ (L6 / E6 / L7 / ICT5) expectations across companies:

Google L6 (Staff SWE): (1) Impact: influences 5+ teams, sets technical direction for a significant area. (2) Scope: designs systems that serve millions of users. (3) Leadership: mentors L3-L5 engineers, leads design reviews. (4) Independence: works with minimal guidance, defines own projects. (5) Google L6 is the highest level that does not require company-wide recognition — you can be a Staff engineer by being the best in your area.

Meta E6 (Staff SWE): (1) Impact: organization-wide (50-100+ engineers). (2) Meta is the most 'IC-heavy' — E6 engineers are expected to code significantly (40-60% of time). (3) Cross-team influence is critical — your work must be adopted by other teams. (4) Beta/Launch mentality — Meta moves fast, and E6 engineers are expected to drive that velocity. (5) E6 is where Meta's 'senior engineer' level starts — E5 is considered mid-level at Meta.

Amazon L7 (Principal Engineer): (1) Amazon's Principal is equivalent to Staff at other companies (Amazon's L6 = Senior). (2) Scope: influences across the organization (100+ engineers). (3) WRITE (Written Narrative) culture — Amazon L7 promotions require a written narrative that demonstrates 4+ Leadership Principles at scale. (4) Bar Raisers — L7 candidates are evaluated by the Bar Raiser process. (5) Amazon has the strongest 'operator' culture — L7 engineers are expected to own operational excellence.

Apple ICT5 (Staff): (1) Craftsmanship focus — the quality of your work matters as much as the impact. (2) Cross-functional collaboration with design, hardware, and product teams. (3) Presentation skills are critical — the technical presentation round is unique to Apple. (4) Apple's 'behind the dot' thinking — not just what you build, but the principles behind it.

Microsoft 65-66 (Principal): (1) Breadth across technical domains — Principal engineers at Microsoft are expected to be proficient in multiple areas. (2) Growth mindset — Microsoft's core cultural value; how you handle feedback and learn from failure. (3) Customer obsession — enterprise-first thinking; every technical decision ties back to customer value. (4) The ASAP (Azure Scale Process) for Principal+ roles — dedicated design discussion at Azure scale.

Staff+ total compensation components: (1) Base salary — typically capped at $200K-$250K for most FAANGs (Meta and Netflix are exceptions with higher caps). (2) Annual bonus — 10-30% of base salary, tied to company and individual performance. (3) RSUs (Restricted Stock Units) — the largest component at Staff+ level. 4-year vesting, typically 25% per year (some companies use monthly or quarterly vesting). Refresh grants are awarded annually. (4) Sign-on bonus — one-time cash payment, typically $50K-$200K, often with a clawback if you leave within 1-2 years. (5) Perks — meals, transportation, health insurance, 401K matching, education stipend.

Typical Staff+ total comp by company (San Francisco, 4-year average): (1) Google L6: $400K-$550K (base $200K-$230K, bonus 15-20%, RSU $800K-$1.2M over 4 years). (2) Meta E6: $450K-$650K (base $200K-$250K, bonus 10%, RSU $1M-$1.8M). Meta is known for aggressive RSU refreshers. (3) Amazon L7: $400K-$600K (base $180K-$220K, bonus year 1/2 are cash-heavy, RSUs back-loaded). Amazon's compensation is back-loaded — year 1-2 comp is lower, year 3-4 comp is higher. (4) Apple ICT5: $400K-$550K (base $200K-$240K, RSUs $600K-$1M, cash bonus 10-15%). (5) Microsoft 65: $300K-$400K (base $180K-$210K, RSUs $400K-$600K, bonus 10-20%). Microsoft's comp has increased significantly but still lags other FAANGs. (6) Netflix IC4: $400K-$600K (all cash — Netflix doesn't use RSUs. You can optionally allocate some comp to stock). Netflix pays top-of-market for each role. (7) Stripe L4-L5: $400K-$600K (base $200K-$230K, RSU value depends on Stripe's valuation). Stripe's pre-IPO stock has significant upside potential.

SAP engineer comp comparison: An SAP Solution Architect at SAP (T6-T8, 15+ years experience) in the US earns $180K-$280K total comp. The same engineer at a FAANG at L6/ICT5 would earn $400K-$600K — a 2-3x increase. This is the 'FAANG premium' and the primary financial motivation for the SAP-to-FAANG transition.

details: FAANG Interview Tip: The leveling translation is critical for setting expectations and negotiating offers. Key takeaways: (1) SAP's 'Architect' title (Solution Architect, Enterprise Architect) maps to L6/ICT5/65 at most FAANGs — you are targeting Staff+ roles. (2) Do NOT accept a 'Senior' level offer (L5/E5) if you are an established architect at SAP — your experience warrants Staff+. (3) If you cannot get a Staff+ interview at Google or Meta, consider Amazon (L7 Principal) or Microsoft (65 Principal) — they value enterprise experience more. (4) The best economic outcomes are at Meta and Netflix (highest total comp for Staff+ ICs). (5) Use the translation table to frame your experience: 'I've operated as a Staff+ equivalent at SAP for X years, leading Y teams and designing Z-scale systems.'
